content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers.

You perform the following operation on it N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 times:


Choose index iii (1‚â§i<‚à£A‚à£1 \le i < |A|1‚â§i<‚à£A‚à£) and let X=Ai+2‚ãÖAi+1X = A_i + 2 \cdot A_{i + 1}X=Ai‚Äã+2‚ãÖAi+1‚Äã.
Delete AiA_iAi‚Äã and Ai+1A_{i + 1}Ai+1‚Äã from the array AAA.
Insert XXX back to the array AAA in the same place.
More formally, you replace array AAA with A[1,i‚àí1]+[X]+A[i+2,N]A[1, i - 1] + [X] + A[i + 2, N]A[1,i‚àí1]+[X]+A[i+2,N], where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] represents the subarray [AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã].

At the end of the N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 operations, only one element remains. What is the minimum and maximum possible value of that last element?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum and maximum possible value of the final element.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We are forced to choose i=1i = 1i=1 in the first and only operation, and XXX is calculated as 252525 which becomes our final element.
Test Case 2 : To achieve the minimum,

First choose i=1i = 1i=1, X=3X = 3X=3, AAA updated to [3,1][3, 1][3,1].
Again choose i=1i = 1i=1, X=5X = 5X=5, AAA updated to [5][5][5].

To achieve the maximum,

First choose i=2i = 2i=2, X=3X = 3X=3, AAA updated to [1,3][1, 3][1,3].
Next choose i=1i = 1i=1, X=7X = 7X=7, AAA updated to [7][7][7].
